outlook.headline: EUV demand broadens as tight supply and High-NA prep persist
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters look better for EUV lithography as the bullish read is broadening across the stack: ASML still sees hot demand for its core platform across multiple nodes, Micron points to higher EUV adoption helping keep supply constrained, and Veeco sees broader adoption plus High-NA prep lifting enabling demand. The main beneficiaries are the EUV tool and enablement ecosystem, while the key thing to watch is whether this tight-supply, adoption-led momentum stays intact as High-NA work advances.

drivers.label: Broader EUV adoption
drivers.detail: More EUV penetration is supporting demand across multiple companies rather than a single end-market.

drivers.label: Tight supply remains constructive
drivers.detail: Constrained supply is still part of the setup, supporting demand for tools and signaling continued need for EUV capacity.

drivers.label: ASML core platform strength across nodes
drivers.detail: Leadership in EUV across multiple nodes keeps demand for the core toolset hot.

drivers.label: High-NA preparation lifts enablers
drivers.detail: High-NA prep is creating incremental demand for mask blank and pellicle technologies.

risks: Tight supply could ease faster than expected, reducing the near-term bullish setup.
A slower-than-expected High-NA transition would temper incremental demand for enablement products.
If EUV adoption broadens more slowly than implied, the current momentum could stall.
